openmeter/meterevent.purpose: Public API surface for listing metered usage events; owns Service interface, domain types (Event, ListEventsParams, ListEventsV2Params), and hard constraints (32-day lookback, 100-event limit). Adapter and HTTP handler sub-packages implement and expose the service.

patterns.name: Params.Validate() before delegation
patterns.description: Both ListEventsParams and ListEventsV2Params expose a Validate() method using errors.Join for multi-error aggregation. Callers (adapter, httphandler) must call Validate() before delegating to streaming.Connector.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: SortBy-aware Cursor emission
patterns.description: Event.Cursor() switches on SortBy (Time/IngestedAt/StoredAt) to pick the correct timestamp field. StoreRowID is always used as tiebreak. New sort fields require a matching case in Cursor().
patterns.example: case streaming.EventSortFieldIngestedAt: return pagination.NewCursor(e.IngestedAt, e.StoreRowID)

patterns.name: ValidationErrors as per-event field
patterns.description: Individual event validation failures are attached to Event.ValidationErrors []error, not returned as a top-level error from the service method.
patterns.example: event.ValidationErrors = append(event.ValidationErrors, validationErr)

patterns.name: CustomerID filter: only $in supported
patterns.description: ListEventsV2Params.Validate() rejects any CustomerID filter that is not empty and does not use .In — enforced by explicit check in Validate().
patterns.example: if !p.CustomerID.IsEmpty() && p.CustomerID.In == nil { errs = append(errs, errors.New("customer id filter supports only in")) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service interface, Event domain type, ListEventsParams, ListEventsV2Params, and the two const limits (MaximumFromDuration, MaximumLimit). Source of truth for validation logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new filter fields requires a matching Validate() clause using ValidateWithComplexity; omitting it lets invalid filters reach ClickHouse.

key_file_guides.role: Single service implementation — delegates to streaming.Connector, enriches results with customer IDs (with caching), and attaches ValidationErrors per event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not import openmeter/ent/db here. Do not emit NextCursor when the result page is smaller than effective limit.

key_file_guides.role: All API↔domain type conversions. Must be the only place where field mappings live.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Inlining conversions in event.go or event_v2.go is an anti-pattern.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db in adapter — this is a streaming-only package
Returning top-level error for individual event validation failures instead of attaching to Event.ValidationErrors
Emitting NextCursor when result page is smaller than the effective limit
Skipping params.Validate() before calling streaming.Connector
Inlining API↔domain conversions in handler files instead of mapping.go

decisions.decision: ValidationErrors are per-event fields, not returned as top-level errors
decisions.rationale: Allows partial results to be returned with inline error annotations rather than failing the entire listing operation for one bad event.

decisions.decision: Hard time-window and count limits (32 days, 100 events) enforced in Validate(), not in adapter or handler
decisions.rationale: Centralising limits in the domain params type makes them enforceable regardless of which caller invokes the service.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new sort field: requires both SortField constant and a Cursor() case

openmeter/meterexport.purpose: Streaming export of pre-aggregated synthetic meter events; converts ClickHouse SUM/COUNT meter rows into RawEvents suitable for re-ingestion. The root package owns the Service interface and config/params types; implementation lives in the service/ sub-package.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: DataExportConfig and DataExportParams both expose Validate() using errors.Join. The service/ sub-package calls Validate() at the start of every public method.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Dual-channel streaming result
patterns.description: ExportSyntheticMeterData writes events to result chan<- streaming.RawEvent and errors to err chan<- error. Channels are owned and closed exclusively by the producing goroutine via defer close.
patterns.example: func (s *svc) ExportSyntheticMeterData(ctx context.Context, params DataExportParams, result chan<- streaming.RawEvent, err chan<- error) error

patterns.name: iter.Seq2 wrapper as preferred caller API
patterns.description: ExportSyntheticMeterDataIter wraps the dual-channel method in an iter.Seq2[streaming.RawEvent, error] that handles channel management and context cancellation internally.
patterns.example: for event, err := range seq { if err != nil { handle(err) }; process(event) }

patterns.name: Custom JSON marshal/unmarshal for time.Location
patterns.description: DataExportConfig implements MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON to serialize ExportWindowTimeZone as a string (IANA name). Nil timezone marshals to empty string and unmarshals back to nil.
patterns.example: func (c DataExportConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) { tzName := ""; if c.ExportWindowTimeZone != nil { tzName = c.ExportWindowTimeZone.String() } ... }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service interface, TargetMeterDescriptor, DataExportConfig, DataExportParams, and their Validate()/JSON methods. Source of truth for supported aggregations constraint (SUM, COUNT only).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new aggregation type requires whitelisting it in service/syntheticdata.go validateAndGetMeter switch AND createEventFromMeterRow.

key_file_guides.role: Windowed pagination logic that drives the ClickHouse query loop and feeds the result channel.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never close resultCh or errCh outside of funnel — channel ownership is exclusive.

key_file_guides.role: iter.Seq2 wrapper — the idiomatic API for callers that want range-based iteration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Early break from range loop must cancel context to stop the producer goroutine.

anti_patterns: Adding a new aggregation type to createEventFromMeterRow without whitelisting in validateAndGetMeter
Closing resultCh or errCh outside the producing funnel goroutine
Calling ExportSyntheticMeterData synchronously without draining both channels — deadlocks on buffer overflow
Adding Ent/DB dependencies — this package depends only on streaming.Connector and meter.Service
Skipping sync.Once guard for context-cancellation errors in errCh — causes duplicate entries

decisions.decision: Dual-channel streaming rather than returning a slice
decisions.rationale: Export can span many ClickHouse window pages; buffering all results in memory before returning would OOM for large time ranges.

decisions.decision: Only SUM and COUNT aggregations supported
decisions.rationale: Pre-aggregated synthetic events are only reversible/reconstructable for additive (SUM) and countable (COUNT) aggregation functions; other types (MAX, UNIQUE_COUNT) lose information when windowed.

openmeter/namespace.purpose: Multi-tenancy infrastructure: Manager fans out CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace to all registered Handler implementations (ClickHouse, Kafka ingest, Ledger). namespacedriver sub-package provides the HTTP-layer NamespaceDecoder abstraction.

patterns.name: Handler registration before CreateDefaultNamespace
patterns.description: Handlers must be registered via RegisterHandler before CreateDefaultNamespace is called at server startup, otherwise the handler misses default namespace provisioning.
patterns.example: manager.RegisterHandler(streamingConnector); manager.RegisterHandler(kafkaCollector); manager.CreateDefaultNamespace(ctx)

patterns.name: Fan-out with errors.Join (no short-circuit)
patterns.description: createNamespace/deleteNamespace iterate all handlers and accumulate errors with errors.Join. A failure in one handler does not prevent others from being called.
patterns.example: for _, handler := range m.config.Handlers { if err := handler.CreateNamespace(ctx, name); err != nil { errs = append(errs, err) } }; return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: Default namespace protected from deletion
patterns.description: DeleteNamespace rejects deletion of the default namespace by comparing name == m.config.DefaultNamespace before any handler call.
patterns.example: if name == m.config.DefaultNamespace { return errors.New("cannot delete default namespace") }

patterns.name: StaticNamespaceDecoder as plain string type
patterns.description: namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder is a named string type, not a struct — cast a string literal to use it.
patterns.example: var decoder namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder = namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder("default")

key_file_guides.role: Full Manager implementation including RWMutex-guarded handler list, RegisterHandler, CreateNamespace, DeleteNamespace, CreateDefaultNamespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No rollback on partial handler failure — a TODO comment documents this. Do not assume atomicity.

key_file_guides.role: NamespaceDecoder interface + StaticNamespaceDecoder. Must stay dependency-free (no domain imports).
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetNamespace returning (empty string, true) silently scopes queries to an unintended tenant.

anti_patterns: Registering a Handler after CreateDefaultNamespace is called — it will miss default namespace provisioning
Returning true with an empty string from NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace
Importing domain packages (billing, customer, entitlement) in namespacedriver — it must stay infrastructure-only
Assuming createNamespace is atomic — failures in one handler do not roll back others
Passing nil as a Handler to NewManager or RegisterHandler — both return an error; callers must check

decisions.decision: Manager holds a RWMutex-guarded handler slice rather than fixed set
decisions.rationale: Handlers are registered dynamically at startup (after Manager construction) to support optional subsystems like Ledger that depend on credits.enabled config flag.

decisions.decision: StaticNamespaceDecoder is a string type, not a struct
decisions.rationale: Self-hosted deployments always route to one namespace; a plain named string avoids unnecessary initialization ceremony while still satisfying the NamespaceDecoder interface.

openmeter/portal.purpose: Issues and validates short-lived HS256 JWT portal tokens scoped to a namespace, subject, and optional meter slug allowlist; ListTokens and InvalidateToken are intentionally unimplemented. The root package owns the Service interface and input/output types.

patterns.name: Input Validate() before service call
patterns.description: CreateTokenInput, ListTokensInput, and InvalidateTokenInput each expose Validate() with errors.Join. The adapter and httphandler must call Validate() before delegating.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: Token field set only in CreateToken response
patterns.description: PortalToken.Token is a *string that is nil in all read paths. The JWT string is assigned only after token creation — never inside toAPIPortalToken mapping.
patterns.example: token, err := adapter.CreateToken(ctx, input); token.Token = &jwtString

patterns.name: Noop adapter for unimplemented methods
patterns.description: ListTokens and InvalidateToken on the JWT adapter return models.NewGenericNotImplementedError. A separate noop.go provides a full no-op stub for test wiring.
patterns.example: func (a *Adapter) ListTokens(...) (pagination.Result[*PortalToken], error) { return pagination.Result[*PortalToken]{}, models.NewGenericNotImplementedError("list portal tokens") }

patterns.name: Meter slug allowlist enforced in httphandler operation, not adapter
patterns.description: CreateToken httphandler validates AllowedMeterSlugs against meter.Service before calling portal.Service.CreateToken.
patterns.example: // In httphandler/portal.go operation func: validate each slug via meterSvc.GetMeterByIDOrSlug

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service interface (composed of PortalTokenService), all input/output types, and Validate() methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AllowedMeterSlugs is *[]string — nil means all slugs allowed, non-nil empty slice means no slugs allowed. Distinguish these cases.

key_file_guides.role: JWT-backed stateless adapter. Config.SharedSecret must be non-empty. Strict JWT parse options must not be relaxed.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not store token state in DB — this adapter is intentionally stateless.

key_file_guides.role: Chi middleware dispatching per OpenAPI security scheme; injects validated subject into context.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Auth failures must always be 401, never 200 or 500.

anti_patterns: Relaxing JWT validation options (removing WithExpirationRequired, WithStrictDecoding)
Setting Token field inside toAPIPortalToken — must only be set in CreateToken response path
Adding DB persistence to the JWT adapter — stateless by design; DB-backed impl would be a new adapter
Skipping AllowedMeterSlugs validation in httphandler when meterSlug param is absent
Returning 500 for auth failures in authenticator — must always be 401

decisions.decision: Stateless JWT tokens with no DB persistence
decisions.rationale: Portal tokens are short-lived and scoped; avoiding a DB table removes a write-path dependency and keeps token issuance fast. ListTokens/InvalidateToken are explicitly unimplemented until a DB-backed adapter is added.

decisions.decision: Meter slug validation in HTTP handler, not portal.Service
decisions.rationale: portal.Service has no dependency on meter.Service; meter existence validation is a cross-domain concern handled at the transport boundary.

openmeter/progressmanager.purpose: Tracks progress of long-running async operations (e.g. ClickHouse export jobs) via Redis-backed key-value store with TTL. Root package defines Service and Adapter interfaces; entity/ sub-package owns domain types; adapter/ provides Redis, noop, and mock implementations.

patterns.name: Validate() on every input type
patterns.description: GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput, and Progress all implement Validate() with errors.Join. Adapter calls Validate() before any Redis operation.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Namespaced Redis key format
patterns.description: Redis keys include namespace and operation ID to prevent multi-tenant collisions. Key format defined in adapter/progress.go getKey().
patterns.example: func getKey(input entity.GetProgressInput) string { return fmt.Sprintf("progress:%s:%s", input.Namespace, input.ID) }

patterns.name: redis.Nil mapped to GenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: When Redis returns redis.Nil (key not found), the adapter maps it to models.GenericNotFoundError — enabling correct HTTP 404 mapping.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, redis.Nil) { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError("progress", input.ID) }

patterns.name: Noop UpsertProgress is a safe silent no-op
patterns.description: adapterNoop.UpsertProgress always returns nil. Callers rely on this for test/disabled-feature wiring.
patterns.example: func (a *adapterNoop) UpsertProgress(_ context.Context, _ entity.UpsertProgressInput) error { return nil }

patterns.name: Service and Adapter interfaces mirror each other
patterns.description: Service embeds ProgressManagerService; Adapter embeds ProgressManagerAdapter — both have identical method signatures. Service is the outward-facing interface; Adapter is the storage contract.
patterns.example: type Service interface { ProgressManagerService }; type Adapter interface { ProgressManagerAdapter }

key_file_guides.role: Defines outward-facing Service interface. Must stay identical to Adapter method signatures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a method to Service without adding it to Adapter (and vice versa) breaks the compile-time interface chain.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Adapter interface — storage contract for the Redis adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service and Adapter are deliberately identical here; do not diverge them.

key_file_guides.role: All domain value types and Validate() methods. Pure types only — no Redis or Ent imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding persistence logic here breaks the entity/adapter separation.

key_file_guides.role: Redis implementation + noop + mock. Key format, TTL, and redis.Nil mapping live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing getKey() format orphans all in-flight progress records with no migration path.

anti_patterns: Storing progress keys without namespace prefix — breaks multi-tenant isolation
Returning raw redis errors instead of models.GenericNotFoundError for redis.Nil
Changing the Redis key format in getKey() without a migration plan
Adding Ent/DB dependencies to entity/ — it must be pure domain types
Making adapterNoop.UpsertProgress return an error — callers rely on noop writes being safe

decisions.decision: Redis (not PostgreSQL/Ent) as the backing store
decisions.rationale: Progress records are ephemeral, TTL-scoped, and high-write; a Redis hash with TTL avoids Postgres table bloat and eliminates the need for a cleanup job.

decisions.decision: Entity types in a separate entity/ sub-package
decisions.rationale: Keeps the root package as a thin interface definition layer; entity types can be imported by adapter/ without creating a circular import with the root package.
